ICtoolkit 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Plot information criterion paths for glmnet or ncvreg objects

Source: R/plot_ic_path.R
plot_ic_path.Rd

Computes one or more information criteria across the full regularisation path of a glmnet or ncvreg object and plots each as a line against a transform of lambda. A vertical dashed line marks the lambda that minimises each criterion.

plot_ic_path(
  fit,
  criteria = c("AIC", "BIC"),
  P = NULL,
  P_index = NULL,
  gamma = "ebic",
  xvar = c("-log(lambda)", "log(lambda)", "lambda"),
  mark_min = TRUE,
  legend = TRUE,
  cols = NULL,
  lwd = 2,
  ...
)

Arguments

fit

A fitted glmnet or ncvreg object.

criteria

Character vector of criteria to plot. One or more of "AIC", "AICc", "BIC", "EBIC", "RBIC". Default c("AIC", "BIC").

P

Integer. Total candidate predictors (excluding intercept). Required when "EBIC" is in criteria.

P_index

Named list of variable groups. Required when "RBIC" is in criteria. See compute_rbic() for format details.

gamma

Gamma specification for EBIC / RBIC. Default "ebic". See compute_ebic() for accepted values.

xvar

What to place on the x-axis. One of "-log(lambda)" (default), "log(lambda)", or "lambda".

mark_min

Logical. Draw a vertical dashed line at the IC-minimising lambda for each criterion. Default TRUE.

legend

Logical. Draw a legend when more than one criterion is plotted. Default TRUE.

cols

Named or unnamed character/integer vector of colours, one per criterion. Defaults to seq_along(criteria) (R's standard palette).

lwd

Line width. Default 2.

...

Additional graphical parameters passed to graphics::plot().

Value

Invisibly returns a list with:

ic

Named list of IC vectors, one element per criterion.

opt_lambda

Named numeric vector of IC-minimising lambda values.

lambda

The full lambda sequence from fit.

See also

compute_aic(), compute_bic(), compute_ebic(), compute_rbic()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
library(glmnet)
set.seed(1)
X <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 20), 100, 20,
            dimnames = list(NULL, paste0("x", 1:20)))
y <- X[, 1] * 2 - X[, 3] + rnorm(100)
fit <- glmnet(X, y)

plot_ic_path(fit, criteria = c("AIC", "BIC", "EBIC"), P = 20)

# RBIC with two predictor groups
P_index <- list(first10 = paste0("x", 1:10), second10 = paste0("x", 11:20))
plot_ic_path(fit, criteria = c("BIC", "RBIC"), P_index = P_index)
} # }
